title: podman rm: fails, and leaves everything hosed
body:
Another hard-to-isolate flake. I'm not convinced that it's truly podman-remote-only, because I've actually seen this one on my laptop. Two symptoms (possibly unrelated):
```
# podman-remote --url unix:/tmp/podman_tmp_loGI rm -t 0 -f cpcontainer
Error: cannot remove container 5d183a4ab677c5b65a78ebf87720153aee486145b24631f08edb7e20671498b7 as it could not be stopped: error sending SIGKILL to container 5d183a4ab677c5b65a78ebf87720153aee486145b24631f08edb7e20671498b7: %!w(<nil>)
[ rc=125 (** EXPECTED 0 **) ]
```
and
```
# podman-remote --url unix:/tmp/podman_tmp_fYnD pod rm -f -t 0 -a
Error: error freeing lock for container 38ecd608db0c5616563d5b268e85fea2fbada7a9b7d57eabf705c4f8f92aca92: no such file or directory
[ rc=125 (** EXPECTED 0 **) ]
```
With the second one, once it happens, the entire system becomes completely unusable.

Here's an [example of the cannot-remove one](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/4550846103945216/html/sys-remote-fedora-36-aarch64-root-host.log.html#t--00140)

Here's an [example of the error-freeing one](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/6115402563452928/html/sys-remote-fedora-36-aarch64-root-host.log.html#t--00266)

comments.body:
Here's a cannot-remove one, [int remote ubuntu root](https://storage.googleapis.com/cirrus-ci-6707778565701632-fcae48/artifacts/containers/podman/6389994871848960/html/int-remote-ubuntu-2204-root-host.log.html#t--podman-run-network-connection-with-default-bridge--1) on August 2

Aha, and here it is [int podman f36 root](https://storage.googleapis.com/cirrus-ci-6707778565701632-fcae48/artifacts/containers/podman/6531448663441408/html/int-podman-fedora-36-root-container.log.html#t--podman-run-network-connection-with-default-bridge--1), confirming that it's not a remote-only bug.

Here's the `error freeing` one, [sys remote f36 rootless](https://storage.googleapis.com/cirrus-ci-6707778565701632-fcae48/artifacts/containers/podman/5555957253013504/html/sys-remote-fedora-36-rootless-host.log.html#t--00364), possibly connected to #11594

The `error freeing` one, [int podman f35 root](https://storage.googleapis.com/cirrus-ci-6707778565701632-fcae48/artifacts/containers/podman/6191567470854144/html/int-podman-fedora-35-root-host.log.html#t--podman-pod-logs-with-container-names--1), May 27.

Here's one with possibly useful context. [sys remote f36 rootless](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/6408248566743040/html/sys-remote-fedora-36-rootless-host.log.html#t--00377):
```
$ podman-remote --url unix:/tmp/podman_tmp_r7Ne kube down /tmp/podman_bats.DD7B1h/test.yaml
Error: json: cannot unmarshal string into Go struct field PodRmReport.RmReport.Err of type error
```
Then, immediately after that, kablooie: `error freeing` on all subsequent tests.

Interesting. This one [sys remote ubuntu-2204 root](https://storage.googleapis.com/cirrus-ci-6707778565701632-fcae48/artifacts/containers/podman/6169165353451520/html/sys-remote-ubuntu-2204-root-host.log.html#t--00270) goes into the `error freeing` death spiral, but the trigger seems to be the `unlinkat/EBUSY` flake (#11594):
```
# podman-remote --url unix:/tmp/podman_tmp_9X4G pod rm yky40edmyy
Error: error removing container c460399c59e569bd25127913cecc01c7de058a07c0aacde4bc9fa2ae2f2c3128 root filesystem: 1 error occurred:
	* unlinkat /var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/a6b8a4bc3e3ab118ed01e0b3e8d16f7b41a7b50176f6f9eb713c5baf9bbf47e0/merged: device or resource busy
[ rc=125 (** EXPECTED 0 **) ]
#/vvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvv
#| FAIL: exit code is 125; expected 0
#\^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
# podman-remote --url unix:/tmp/podman_tmp_9X4G pod rm -f -t 0 -a
Error: error freeing lock for container c460399c59e569bd25127913cecc01c7de058a07c0aacde4bc9fa2ae2f2c3128: no such file or directory
```

Here's another `unlinkat` (#11594) followed by systemsnafu: [sys remote ubuntu 2204 root](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/6260854717939712/html/sys-remote-ubuntu-2204-root-host.log.html).

@containers/podman-maintainers since this is now in the wild, I'd say it's higher priority.
[f36 aarch64 root](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/5872726341582848/html/sys-remote-fedora-36-aarch64-root-host.log.html)
No `podman rm` that I can see, but here we go again, [remote f36 root](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/4874587107229696/html/sys-remote-fedora-36-root-host.log.html)
[remote f36-aarch64 root](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/5872726341582848/html/sys-remote-fedora-36-aarch64-root-host.log.html#t--00267)
[remote f36 root](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/5094388970815488/html/sys-remote-fedora-36-root-host.log.html#t--00265)
[remote f36-aarch64 root](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/4812913994104832/html/sys-remote-fedora-36-aarch64-root-host.log.html#t--00247)

Here's another one with the `unmarshal` error happening first, then everything going bonkers. [remote ubuntu root](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/5080405429714944/html/sys-remote-ubuntu-2204-root-host.log.html#t--00384)
```
# podman-remote --url unix:/tmp/podman_tmp_wtPS inspect --format {{.HostConfig.LogConfig.Type}} test_pod-test
journald
# podman-remote --url unix:/tmp/podman_tmp_wtPS kube down /tmp/podman_bats.LQsRLR/test.yaml
Error: json: cannot unmarshal string into Go struct field PodRmReport.RmReport.Err of type error
...
# podman-remote --url unix:/tmp/podman_tmp_wtPS pod rm -t 0 -f -a
Error: removing container d4d4bc7ee1505bd7738922c686fd35788c67e4c7a936e62044287dde0abaf6a1 root filesystem: 1 error occurred:
	* unlinkat /var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/c01ffe8ae42779fe4737be67fb350cabbe5f0c0a309ffbcd318fd584ff7af701/merged: device or resource busy
```

Interesting variation on [remote f36-aarch64 root](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/5694475040194560/html/sys-remote-fedora-36-aarch64-root-host.log.html#t--00124): "cannot remove container" but subsequent tests actually pass:
```
# podman-remote --url unix:/tmp/podman_tmp_IPZS kill cpcontainer
cpcontainer
# podman-remote --url unix:/tmp/podman_tmp_IPZS rm -t 0 -f cpcontainer
Error: cannot remove container 380fcd0fd9264c2daf9157c7dfe07a8f7e31a84bd1c47e14a3ad01e76d4a7682 as it could not be stopped: sending SIGKILL to container 380fcd0fd9264c2daf9157c7dfe07a8f7e31a84bd1c47e14a3ad01e76d4a7682: container state improper
[ rc=2 (** EXPECTED 0 **) ]
```

Another one like yesterday's, again on [remote f36-aarch64 root](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/5880092604760064/html/sys-remote-fedora-36-aarch64-root-host.log.html#t--00126), again just one `rm` failure without the cascade of death:
```
# podman-remote --url unix:/tmp/podman_tmp_BY5k rm -t 0 -f cpcontainer
Error: cannot remove container X as it could not be stopped: sending SIGKILL to container X: container state improper
```

Here's the unlinkat/ebusy one on [f36 remote rootless](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/5022141530767360/html/sys-remote-fedora-36-rootless-host.log.html#t--00346)

OK, f2f over, we need serious attention on this one.
[f36 remote rootless](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/5193909100871680/html/sys-remote-fedora-36-rootless-host.log.html#t--00390) again

[f36 remote root](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/6520997825216512/html/sys-remote-fedora-36-root-host.log.html#t--00266)

Re-adding `remote` tag which was "helpfully" removed by bot.

Slightly different symptom here, also [f36 remote root](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/6520997825216512/html/sys-remote-fedora-36-root-host.log.html#t--00266):
```
#  podman-remote run --rm --pod podOWvA8g8Ywr quay.io/libpod/testimage:20220615 ls /dev/shm/test1
/dev/shm/test1
time="2022-09-30T07:21:59-05:00" level=error msg="Removing container SHA: 
   container \"SHA\" is mounted and cannot be removed without using force: container state improper"
```
It's followed by the usual cascade of everything-is-broken-forever failures, so I'm treating it as the same bug.
[f36 remote aarch64 root](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/5818176876314624/html/sys-remote-fedora-36-aarch64-root-host.log.html)

Another new failure mode. Seen in [remote f36-aarch64 root](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/5818176876314624/html/sys-remote-fedora-36-aarch64-root-host.log.html#t--00360):
```
# podman-remote rm -t 0 --all --force --ignore
# podman-remote ps --all --external --format {{.ID}} {{.Names}}
# podman-remote images --all --format {{.Repository}}:{{.Tag}} {{.ID}}
Error: retrieving parent of image "SHA1": 
     you may need to remove the image to resolve the error: 
     unlinkat /var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/SHA2/merged: device or resource busy
```
In this one, there's no `podman rm` that I can see (no failing one, I mean). The failure happens on `podman images`. (And yes, as with all other instances, causes every subsequent test to die).

Can someone please treat this one as serious?

Another one starting with `podman images`, not `podman rm` (AFAICT). In [remote f36 aarch64](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/5054141855694848/html/sys-remote-fedora-36-aarch64-root-host.log.html#t--00272). The title of this issue seems more and more misleading. But there's a `podman rm` just before the `podman images`, so I'm not changing the title yet.

And here's another maybe-related-maybe-not, it happens in `kube down`, in almost the very last test, so I can't quickly tell if the system gets hosed or not. [podman ubuntu-2204 rootless](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/6119046214057984/html/sys-podman-ubuntu-2204-rootless-host.log.html#t--00392). This could be the unlinkat bug instead. They may be related. I really have no idea.
The symptoms look similar to https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/11594, don't they?
Sometimes, but not enough for me to combine the two issues. #11594 is a one-shot: it happens, and life goes on. This issue is a catastrophe: once it happens, podman never works again.

Potentially interesting correlation: #16154 (unmarshal blah blah, podman-remote only) seems to be highly correlated with this one. My gut feeling is that "unmarshal" is its own bug; that what's happening is that THIS issue (the forever-hose) is triggering, and sending an error that podman-remote cannot deal with. So the underlying error is actually this one. But please take this with a grain of salt.

Hard to know if [this one](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/5256793547341824/html/sys-podman-ubuntu-2204-root-host.log.html#t--00392) (ubuntu root) is the hosed flake or the unlinkat/EBUSY one, but I'm going to add it here.

Another case where the first failure presents with "blah blah is mounted", then cascades from there into disaster:
```
not ok 361 podman run with slirp4ns adds correct dns address to resolv.conf
...
# podman-remote run --rm  \
      --network slirp4netns:cidr=172.24.205.0/24 \
      quay.io/libpod/testimage:20221018 \
      grep 172.24.205 /etc/resolv.conf
nameserver 172.24.205.3
time="2022-10-25T13:47:23Z" level=error msg="Removing container SHA: 
         container \"SHA\" is mounted and cannot be removed without using force: container state improper"
...
[all subsequent tests fail]
```
[ubuntu 2204 root remote](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/5336868848926720/html/sys-remote-ubuntu-2204-root-host.log.html#t--00361)

Can we please, please get some attention on this? It's a bad one, flaking at least once per day.

* fedora-36 : sys remote fedora-36 root host [remote]
  * PR #16459
    * [11-09 14:51](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/4900286095949824/html/sys-remote-fedora-36-root-host.log.html#t--00271)
* ubuntu-2204 : sys podman ubuntu-2204 root host
  * PR #16465
    * [11-09 17:04](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/5121353766928384/html/sys-podman-ubuntu-2204-root-host.log.html#t--00393)
* ubuntu-2204 : sys remote ubuntu-2204 root host [remote]
  * PR #16311
    * [10-27 15:03](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/6399263438012416/html/sys-remote-ubuntu-2204-root-host.log.html#t--00361)
@containers/podman-maintainers PTAL
Now seen in [f37 gating tests, too](https://artifacts.dev.testing-farm.io/2fb0707b-d2f9-43f5-9ee0-cfec3e4acbbd/work-tests.ymlM4I85_/tests-GyiFPE/test.podman-remote-rootless.bats.log)

...also can't remove a pod after, being lazy, and attempting rm -f on a running pod.
  
`"Error: error freeing lock for container 95de07970b6cd967f1587294ffe05db767cd2f20bfd061633b010bb3654e2d73: no such file or directory"
`
How can I help?

@Johnpc123 to help you out, i was able to remove the pod that i borked like you did by removing it through the cockpit-podman web interface. it threw that error at me but was able to remove it completely nonetheless, while `podman pod rm gitea -f` couldn't

@mtrmac I think your recent c/storage fixes may have resolved this issue.  It seems to happen only with remote which somehow made me think about your in-process fixes.

WDYT?

This is quite long, and might well mix different root causes:
- `error sending SIGKILL to container 5d183a4ab677c5b65a78ebf87720153aee486145b24631f08edb7e20671498b7: %!w(<nil>)`:
    - the `nil` part was fixed in 6bf8670b694278e5e53655ad40605d099f7ae02b
    - The error itself seems unlikely to be directly c/storage-related ; c/storage does not record PIDs or process state.
- `freeing lock for container`: These are `libpod/lock.Manager` locks
- `unlinkat /var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/…/merged: device or resource busy`: something is missing an unmount, or otherwise mismanaging recorded state vs. running processes??
- `podman-remote images … retrieving parent of image … unlinkat /var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/SHA2/merged: device or resource busy`: Why is obtaining a parent (pure metadata) doing any overlay operations at all?
- “The issue is the timesMounted, err := r.store.Mounted(ctr.ID) call below could be getting screwed up”: That API is _fundamentally_ racy; as far as c/storage cares, the caller is not holding any lock and the data can be out-of date already at the moment it is returned by c/storage. Compare https://github.com/containers/storage/pull/1346/commits/cbc2ca843648d1ed547f9921cf9bbc6eaa2de5c7 . It may well be the case that Podman is doing more synchronization, I wouldn’t know.

In summary nothing of the above is obviously related to the c/storage work I was doing. The _container_ state cleanup (unlike layer state cleanup) is very minimal in c/storage.

My hunch would be to follow the `define.ErrCtrStateInvalid` inconsistencies. Not that I know how.
Thanks for taking a look, @mtrmac !
